1:52689.name: deepa joined SwaaS in 2016 and has spent nearly a decade building the HR function, growing alongside the organization through every stage of its scale. With an MBA from the University of Madras and hands-on expertise in recruiting, onboarding, and performance management, she handles the day-to-day people operations that keep a 200+ strong team engaged and productive. Her decade at SwaaS means she understands the culture, the talent profile, and what good looks like for this team. As Head of HR, she translates that institutional knowledge into hiring decisions and people processes that consistently raise the bar.
1:52689.characters:
deepa joined SwaaS in 2016 and has spent nearly a decade building the HR function, growing alongside the organization through every stage of its scale. 

With an MBA from the University of Madras and hands-on expertise in recruiting, onboarding, and performance management, she handles the day-to-day people operations that keep a 200+ strong team engaged and productive.

1:52557.name: vijayarani brings 25 years of HR leadership spanning technology companies of every size, from early-stage startups to established mid-market firms. At Saksoft, she scaled the organization from 18 to 530 people, an experience that shaped how she thinks about talent, culture, and what it takes to without losing the culture. She spent over a decade as a strategic HR advisor before joining SwaaS as VP HR in 2023. At SwaaS, she leads the people function — ensuring the team, the culture, and the hiring standards hold as the company scales.

1:52513.name: parimal's 20-year career moved from software engineering through project management to delivery leadership, including managing $10M enterprise programs at Enzen with 0 unscheduled downtime and leading a 150-member team at Scan-IT. A PRINCE2 Practitioner and Certified ScrumMaster, he brings both formal governance rigour and hands-on Agile methodology to manage complex, multi-stream deliveries. As VP Project Delivery, he owns the full delivery lifecycle, ensuring every engagement runs to the standard clients expect.

1:52425.name: karthikeyan's 18-year career moved from software engineering into technical management, where he spent 12 years at Defiance Technologies leading large-scale projects across development and support. His delivery record earned consistent recognition, including a Spot Award for Delivery at SAP Labs and multiple team and individual performance awards. He joined SwaaS in 2022 as AVP Delivery, bringing expertise in RPA, process governance, and cross-functional team leadership. At SwaaS, he owns the delivery function, ensuring projects ship on time, within scope, and to the standard clients expect.

1:52381.name: Magesh is a Chartered Accountant who began his career running Sarbanes-Oxley audits for Unilever across Europe and China before leading internal controls at Ford Motor Company India. That foundation in audit and process engineering brought a financial discipline to SwaaS that most technology companies don't build until much later. He joined SwaaS in 2011 to head Finance and Implementation, building the operational and financial infrastructure the company needed to scale. As CFO, he brings the same methodical rigour to the commercial and operational backbone of every engagement

1:52337.name: Murali's career began in code before evolving through business analysis, pre-sales, and delivery management across 25+ years and multiple technology firms. That rare path from developer to GTM strategist, gives him a perspective most marketers don't have: he understands what SwaaS builds before deciding how to position it. At SwaaS for over 8 years, he built the marketing and pre-sales function from scratch. As CMO, he leads the company's B2B growth strategy, content programmes, and digital presence across global markets.

1:52469.name: praveen's 20-year career in enterprise software sales has been built entirely across North America, moving from account management at Kumaran Systems and CSDC to VP of Sales at SwaaS, where he led North American revenue for over seven years. His time as Senior Sales Executive at UiPath gave him deep exposure to the enterprise automation market, a perspective he brought directly back to SwaaS when he rejoined as CRO in 2024. Based in Toronto, he is SwaaS's commercial presence in North America, owning the client relationships and pipeline that drive growth in the company's most important market.

key: SwaaS started in 2008 building software for pharma companies. In that world, there's no room for error. Either you get it right, or people notice. That stuck with us. Seventeen years later, we've grown into something bigger. Now we help VCs, Founders, and Enterprises - ideate, build and scale enterprise software solutions. We're 200+ people building products the right way—with ownership, quality, and accountability. But how we work hasn't changed. Good technology comes down to balance: Technology, People, Process, Time, and Cost. Getting that mix right is what separates great products from broken ones. It's also where our name comes from. SwaaS draws on Swaasam (Tamil) and Swasa (Sanskrit), both meaning breath, the steady rhythm that keeps everything alive. We try to bring that same steadiness to the products and the teams we build. We've seen what happens when companies cut corners with offshore teams. Broken code. Missed deadlines. Teams building the wrong thing. We learned from watching that happen. So we built a different way.0

value.characters:
SwaaS started in 2008 building software for pharma companies. In that world, there's no room for error. Either you get it right, or people notice. That stuck with us. 

Seventeen years later, we've grown into something bigger. Now we help VCs, Founders, and Enterprises - ideate, build and scale enterprise software solutions. We're 200+ people building products the right way with ownership, quality, and accountability. But how we work hasn't changed. 

Good technology comes down to balance: Technology, People, Process, Time, and Cost. Getting that mix right is what separates great products from broken ones. It's also where our name comes from. SwaaS draws on Swaasam (Tamil) and Swasa (Sanskrit), both meaning breath, the steady rhythm that keeps everything alive. We try to bring that same steadiness to the products and the teams we build. 

We've seen what happens when companies cut corners with offshore teams. Broken code. Missed deadlines. Teams building the wrong thing. We learned from watching that happen. So we built a different way.
Successfully launched a Mobile consumer experience for a leading Middle Eastern Bank

1:52601.name: Manikandan began his career as an Audit Executive at NRG Associates before joining SwaaS in 2012, making him one of the longest-serving members of the finance team. A qualified Cost Accountant with 14+ years of experience, his expertise spans financial analysis, MIS (Management Information Systems), and treasury management. At SwaaS, he has played a direct role in the company's global expansion, including the setup of its US subsidiary, while managing financial relationships with banks, auditors, and external consultants. As Senior Manager Finance, he oversees cash flow monitoring and fund optimization to keep SwaaS's growth on solid financial ground.

1:50348.name: SwaaS started in 2008 building software for pharma companies. In that world, there's no room for error. Either you get it right, or people notice. That stuck with us. Seventeen years later, we've grown into something bigger. Now we help VCs, Founders, and Enterprises - ideate, build and scale enterprise software solutions. We're 200+ people building products the right way—with ownership, quality, and accountability. But how we work hasn't changed. Good technology comes down to balance: Technology, People, Process, Time, and Cost. Getting that mix right is what separates great products from broken ones. It's also where our name comes from. SwaaS draws on Swaasam (Tamil) and Swasa (Sanskrit), both meaning breath, the steady rhythm that keeps everything alive. We try to bring that same steadiness to the products and the teams we build. We've seen what happens when companies cut corners with offshore teams. Broken code. Missed deadlines. Teams building the wrong thing. We learned from watching that happen. So we built a different way.
1:50348.characters:
SwaaS started in 2008 building software for pharma companies. In that world, there's no room for error. Either you get it right, or people notice. That stuck with us. 

Seventeen years later, we've grown into something bigger. Now we help VCs, Founders, and Enterprises - ideate, build and scale enterprise software solutions. We're 200+ people building products the right way—with ownership, quality, and accountability. But how we work hasn't changed. 

Good technology comes down to balance: Technology, People, Process, Time, and Cost. Getting that mix right is what separates great products from broken ones. It's also where our name comes from. SwaaS draws on Swaasam (Tamil) and Swasa (Sanskrit), both meaning breath, the steady rhythm that keeps everything alive. We try to bring that same steadiness to the products and the teams we build. 

We've seen what happens when companies cut corners with offshore teams. Broken code. Missed deadlines. Teams building the wrong thing. We learned from watching that happen. So we built a different way.

1:52293.name: anand's career spans 30 years of engineering, consulting, and company building, starting with aviation software at a TCS-Singapore Airlines joint venture before moving into financial services at Satyam, Barclays Capital, and Standard Chartered Bank. At Barclays Capital, he led offshore development centres for FX Derivatives, Money Markets, and Commodities, environments where delivery failure was simply not an option. He joined SwaaS in 2014 and has grown it organically and profitably over the past decade, while remaining hands-on in engineering. As CEO, he brings the delivery discipline of global financial markets engineering to every engagement.
1:52293.characters:
anand's career spans 30 years of engineering, consulting, and company building, starting with aviation software at a TCS-Singapore Airlines joint venture before moving into financial services at Satyam, Barclays Capital, and Standard Chartered Bank. 

At Barclays Capital, he led offshore development centres for FX Derivatives, Money Markets, and Commodities, environments where delivery failure was simply not an option. 

He joined SwaaS in 2014 and has grown it organically and profitably over the past decade, while remaining hands-on in engineering. As CEO, he brings the delivery discipline of global financial markets engineering to every engagement.
